description: User manually hunts for disk hogs (pip cache, stale venvs, ONNX exports, ISOs) across multiple sessions instead of having automated pruning.
Kindle-to-Calibre USB connection breaks repeatedly, requiring manual udev rule recreation each time.
User manually runs drift analysis comparing live Mint system state to NixOS config across multiple sessions, with no scheduled automation.
User wants automated monitoring of available transcription models to stay on the best option, currently done by manual checking.
User manually reloads systemd services after pushing code changes to repos like Autodoro, instead of having post-push hooks.
